question: The longest-lived vertebrate is named after an island.  According to Wikipedia as of January 1, 2021, what is the 2020 estimated population of that island, to the nearest thousand?

towns and settlements\n58\nUnknown outside towns and settlements 12\n\n\x0cGreenland\n\nThe World’s largest island\nGreenland is geographically located on the North American\ncontinent. In terms of geopolitics, however, it is a part of Eu-\nrope. 81 per cent of Greenland is covered by ice, and the total\npopulation is just about 56,000, on an area 1/6 of Siberia´s.\nComprised entirely of vast tundra, Greenland has the world´s\nlowest population density. Counting the ice-free areas only, it\nis a mere 0.3 persons per square kilometer.\n\nGreenland is a self-governing region within the Kingdom of\nDenmark. In 1721, Greenland became a Danish colony, in\n1953 a Danish County, and in 1979, home rule was granted.\nSelf-government was established on June 21th 2009.\n\nAlong with Denmark, Greenland was an EU member from\n1973 on. Following a referendum in 1982, Greenland left the\nunion in 1985 and has not been a member since.\n\nMost of the Greenlandic landscape is pristine tundra. All\ntowns and settlements are located along the coast line. No\nroads exist between towns, and all travel is by ship and air-\ncraft only. Most of the population lives on the southern west\ncoast. This includes the capital Nuuk with around 18,000 in-\nhabitants.\n\nGreenland has its own national flag, and issues its own\nstamps, but does not have a national currency. Danish Krone\n(DKK) is legal tender.\n\nMads Phil - Visit Greenland\n\nSiorapaluk, the northernmost settlement in the\nworld, is located in North West Greenland, a\nmere 1,400 km from the North Pole. Gunn-\nbjørn Fjeld, Greenland\'s highest mountain with\n3,700 meters, is located in East Greenland.\n\n5\n\n\x0cPolitics\n\nThe Inatsisartut (Parliament of Greenland), with 31 members,\nand Naalakkersuisut (the Government) are both located in the\ncapital Nuuk. The Head of Naalakkersuisut is termed “Naalak-\nkersuisut Siulittaasuat”. The remaining members are termed\n“Naalakkersuisut”.\n\nEvery four years, elections for the Inatsisartut are held. A can-\ndidate can run for a party or as an independent. The D´Hondt\nmethod of seat calculation is used.
